rule_text: When the lowest tender cannot supply the full quantity within delivery period in AP stores procurement, the order for the BALANCE may be placed with the next-higher tenderer(s) able to supply within the period — at different rates if necessary — until full quantity is covered, subject to the prescribed monetary limit.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation II(5)
verification_method: If L2/L3 awarded part of order: check L1-quantity-shortfall justification on file + balance award is within delegated monetary limit

rule_text: AP Heads of Departments are authorised to grant 5% price preference to products manufactured in AP State; the preference applies only when stores meet specifications and prices fall within the 5% concession; the preference does NOT apply to local agents or middlemen for out-of-State products.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation II(6) Note — Memo No 8410/Accts/62-11 dated 28-2-1962
verification_method: Check tender BDS contains 5% price-preference clause for AP-manufactured products + local-agent/middleman exclusion

rule_text: AP Article 125 Rule I — preference order in stores procurement when quality and price are equal: (1) articles produced in India from Indian raw materials, (2) articles wholly/partially manufactured in India, (3) imported articles held in Indian stock, (4) specially-imported foreign articles; specially authorised purchasing officers may give an additional price preference up to 5% to Indian-produced/manufactured articles.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule I
verification_method: Check tender BDS evaluation methodology applies the 4-tier preference order; reject if tender favours imports without justification

rule_text: All articles required for AP public service shall be purchased on the condition that DELIVERY shall be made in India for PAYMENT in RUPEES in India (except articles of special/unusual character covered by Rule VIII).
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule II
verification_method: Check tender ITB: delivery in India + payment in INR clause; reject foreign-currency-payment terms unless Rule VIII exemption documented

rule_text: Sales tax (now GST) must be EXCLUDED from the price comparison when purchasing AP stores — the purchasing officer compares tender rates net of tax to arrive at competitive prices, then places orders.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule I Instruction 4 — Memo No 23015-B/544/68-5 dated 7-9-1968
verification_method: Check L1 evaluation explicitly compared rates net of GST/sales tax; reject if tax-inclusive comparison was used
